name: Sarah Vaughan
abstract: Sarah Lois Vaughan (,  March 27, 1924 – April 3, 1990) was an American jazz singer and pianist. Nicknamed "Sassy", "The Divine One", and the "Queen of Bebop", she won two Grammy Awards, including the Lifetime Achievement Award, and was nominated for a total of nine Grammy Awards.

about_quotes.speaker: Roland Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: Sarah Vaughan called. Her bass player, Richard Davis, had recommended me to her and I went out with her. And that was fantastic. That was the greatest experience I ever had musically up to that time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roland Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 15, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Wofford
about_quotes.evidence: I was with her two years, two separate years. I worked with her 12 months in ‘79 and then left and went back in ‘83 and worked another full year.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Wofford interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I don't recognize the people in that band... Woody Herman and Sarah Vaughan, and the 3 Blazers.

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie Cain and Roy Kral
about_quotes.evidence: Sarah Vaughan was a very big influence on me in the early, when I was first working with Roy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie Cain and Roy Kral interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Rowles
about_quotes.evidence: I made a record with Sass, Sarah, 'Sarah Vaughan with Jimmy Rowles' Rhythm Section.'

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Lellis
about_quotes.evidence: my first influences were Mel Torme, Sinatra, Ella, Sarah Vaughan, Carmen McRae...
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Lellis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 31, 2007
